channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Obstruction of pedestrian walk way, 16th April
item.description:
I am writing to raise a concern regarding the use of public pavement space outside Snug Bite on Bromley High Street just after the Bromley Central Library. The business has extended its outdoor seating area in a way that significantly obstructs the side section of the pavement. This particular stretch is often used by pedestrians like me who wish to avoid the more crowded central walkway due to social anxiety. However, due to the current setup, the majority of this side path is no longer accessible, forcing pedestrians to divert into already busy areas causing unnecessary queues during weekends, waiting for mobility scooter, pushchairs or pedestrians with shopping to pass. This creates difficulty for a number of people, including those with mobility scooters, parents with pushchairs, and individuals carrying shopping. It also impacts those who rely on less crowded spaces for personal wellbeing, as the alternative route becomes congested and uncomfortable. At busier times, the reduced space makes it challenging for pedestrians to pass safely and comfortably, increasing the risk of congestion and potential accidents even for those with mobility and sight problems. I have taken this pictures before rush hour because I did not want public faces shown in rush hour on the photos. I would be grateful if this matter could be reviewed to ensure that the pavement remains safely accessible for public use, in line with regulations around obstructions and pedestrian access. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 95, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped furniture, 16th April
item.description:
This cupboard outside weeks court has been dumped and has been there for over a week. Please can it be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thayers Farm Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hard rubbish, 16th April
item.description:
Hard rubbish yet again

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Colvestone Crescent, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cleaning needed in Osborn Terr, 16th April
item.description:
Hi there, some volunteers nicely clean up the quaggy river. The bin was picked up but some rubbish have still not been picked up since last week. If you can please action as some of the rubbish contain medical waste from the publicly known crazy old lady who dumped everything in the quaggy river or intoxicate dogs in Greenwich park by mixing raw meat and pills. Also if you can please clean the full area and not just a section as some rubbish have been there since 2019. Thanks for your help

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Meadowcourt Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Between 51 Chichester Road and St. Mungos, 16th April
item.description:
Household bin bags with food, nappies, etc. have been left in the middle of the pavement. This not only blocks access, forcing pushchairs and wheelchairs into the road, but attracts vermin like rats and foxes

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chichester Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Tree hit by b and q lorry, 16th April
item.description:
Tree hanging across pavement and road - hit by lorry spoke to council asked for photo to be sent as part of registration number shown . Emailed but email couldn’t be delivered so sent via this app

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Selsey Crescent, West Heath, Greater London

item.title: Untaxed abandoned vehicle left on private property, 16th April
item.description:

item.title: Old sofa dumped on pavement, 16th April

Sofa has been dumped on the pavement for several weeks. It is an eyesore.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Champion Grove, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Oil on pavement, 16th April
item.description:
There is often oil leaking onto the pavement from the side gate on the corner, presumably cooking oil from the kebab shop. Today there was more than normal making the pavement very slippery and hazardous, especially for the school children who use the street. The kebab shop should be disposing of their oil properly, not letting it leak onto the pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marlow Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
